tasks.description: Wrap 10 canvas-view.tsx handler functions in React.useCallback to prevent unnecessary FlowCanvas re-renders
acceptance_criteria: All 10 handler functions (handleStepSelect, handleSectionSelect, handleStepUpdate, handleStepCreate, handleStepDelete, handleSectionCreate, handleSectionUpdate, handleSectionDelete, handleConnectionCreate, handleConnectionDelete) wrapped in React.useCallback
Dependency arrays correct — setState functions are stable, state values that are read inside closures are deps
No behavioral regression — handlers still update state correctly

learnings_relevant: IMP-032 already added React.memo to StepNode/SectionNode in iter 101

tasks.special_instructions: Lines 67-100 approximately. Most handlers only use setState (stable reference) so deps arrays will be minimal. handleStepDelete also filters connections and checks selectedStepId — include selectedStepId as dep. handleSectionDelete checks selectedSectionId — include it. Be careful with handleConnectionCreate and handleConnectionDelete which may reference state.

tasks.description: Make Step Usage list items in tool-detail-panel.tsx clickable — navigate to step on canvas
acceptance_criteria: Step names in Step Usage section of tool-detail-panel.tsx are clickable
Clicking a step name navigates to the canvas tab containing that step
Cursor changes to pointer on hover, text styled as link (blue/underline or matching tool-analysis-view.tsx pattern)

patterns_to_follow: tool-analysis-view.tsx already has clickable step links with router.push — match that pattern

tasks.special_instructions: Check tool-analysis-view.tsx for the existing step link pattern (router.push to canvas tab). The tool-detail-panel needs access to workspaceId and the step's tab_id. The step data returned from step-tools API may include tab_id via section join or the step may have section_id which links to a tab. Check the Step type and step-tools API response shape. If tab_id is not available, may need to fetch sections to determine tab_id, or use a simpler approach if steps already carry section_id + sections are available in context.

tasks.description: Add explanatory tooltip to disabled AI Regenerate button when no steps exist
acceptance_criteria: When hasSteps is false, Regenerate button shows tooltip: 'Add steps to the canvas before running AI analysis'
Tooltip uses title attribute or Radix Tooltip (match existing pattern in the file)
Button remains disabled with cursor-not-allowed styling

patterns_to_follow: gap-analysis-view.tsx has similar disabled button with hint text pattern (BUG-027 fix)

tasks.special_instructions: Simple change. Use title attribute on the button wrapper or a hint text below — match the pattern used in gap-analysis-view.tsx for the disabled Generate Summary button.

shared_files.note: No shared file modifications needed for any task

testing_plan.regression_reason: Last regression was iter 134 (2 iterations ago). Risk score 2. Not due per 8-iteration cadence.
